# Codex Build Notes

## 2026-07-17 board-first second-order handoff

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_bouquet_hud_consequence.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: activating the restored Round 1 ceremony's sole `Next Order` action temporarily stacked the greenhouse transition cue, expanded four-goal objective row, and Round 2 tutorial pill above the unchanged `480x480` desktop altar. At exact `1280x720`, the board moved to `303..783px`, cut the eighth row below the viewport, and presented two competing instructions for the first Cursed Thorn lesson.
- Player-visible result: the existing transition cue now owns one compact instruction during the `2.2s` handoff: `Restored Greenhouse · Moonlit Wreath · Match beside thorns`. The tutorial pill stays hidden until that cue yields, and the accepted compact Round 2 objective geometry remains active throughout. No board, tile, objective, move budget, copy surface, control, save field, mechanic, or progression surface was added or resized.
- Immediate-play contract: a committed adjacent pair in Round 2 retires the handoff before the interaction speaks. Pointer, keyboard, and exact-mobile touch can therefore execute the authored thorn swap during the transition, spend exactly one move, clear all three blockers, and settle `Cursed Thorn 3/3 Sealed` without waiting for the timer. The boundary is Round 2-specific, so the replay reinvestment handoff retains its established lifecycle and two-cycle economy trace.
- Measured geometry: desktop handoff samples at `150ms` and `1700ms` retain the exact `480x480` altar at `235..715px`, all eight complete rows, and one transition cue; after `3500ms`, the existing tutorial owns the sole instruction and the altar is `224..704px`. Exact `390x844` mobile retains its `378x378` altar at `328..706px` during the handoff and `322..700px` afterward, with eight complete rows and no horizontal overflow.
- Permanent browser contract: desktop pointer, desktop keyboard, and exact-mobile touch cases sample the transition near `150ms`, `1700ms`, and after `3500ms`; assert one instruction surface, 64 tiles/eight authoritative and complete rows, first-viewport containment, no cue/objective collision, and no image/overflow/runtime failures; then exercise the authored thorn pair before timeout and verify the sealed goal plus reload persistence.
- Verification: the focused handoff matrix passed `3/3`, the complete HUD/failure/handoff matrix passed `14/14`, and the exact two-cycle economy regression passed with unchanged first and second cycle balances. The first integrated run exposed that the newly sealed thorn badge became an implicit third grid row after an immediate swap retired the handoff; the existing badge is now absolutely positioned inside its compact chip, matching the accepted mobile treatment. The corrected thorn-feedback suite passed `3/3`, and the final exact serial Chromium gate passed `96/96` in `19.9m`.
- Final local gates: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, the HTML shape verifier, every spec and extracted inline JavaScript syntax check, `git diff --check`, and the changed-line credential/tracker/network scan passed. Runtime remained `510` nodes, `84` images, 64 tiles/eight rows, three animated elements, two meaningful progress bars, no dormant prototype scaffold, no horizontal overflow, and no visible broken images. Exact-mobile guided swaps returned control in `380ms` and `511ms`.
- Visual evidence inspected: `work/round-two-handoff-desktop-pointer-settled.png` and `work/round-two-handoff-mobile390-touch-settled.png` retain the compact four-goal row, one thorn instruction, complete altar frame, readable sockets, and board-first hierarchy. Deployment, byte parity, and public-host evidence follow below.

## 2026-07-17 completed Cursed Thorn goal retirement

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_pass3_feedback.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_dead_board_reweave.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: the authored Round 2 triple-thorn swap correctly advanced the blocker objective to `Cursed Thorn 3/3`, but the chip retained the same neutral brown treatment as unfinished goals. Dense active play therefore continued to present a completed blocker as work still requiring attention.
- Player-visible result: the existing Cursed Thorn objective now enters the same moss/aged-gold `.complete` grammar as completed flower targets and shows one compact `Sealed` marker inside its current chip. Unfinished `0/3` and partial `2/3` states remain neutral. No objective row, node family, animation, control, mechanic, save field, move budget, or progression surface changed.
- Accessibility contract: the existing chip now exposes `Cursed Thorn goal sealed, 3 of 3 cleared` when complete and an exact progress label while unfinished. The state is not live, so ordinary reloads remain quiet. Existing completion logic continues to remove thorn-specific guidance once sealed.
- Permanent browser contract: the natural desktop `1280x720` and exact mobile `390x844` Round 1 restoration into the authored Round 2 triple-thorn lesson now verifies neutral `0/3` and `2/3`, three localized BREAK events, settled sealed `3/3`, compact seal/count containment, unchanged objective-row and altar geometry, two quiet reloads, no stale thorn hints, and simultaneous `Moves 3` readability. The existing all-goals completion path still proceeds to one focused Upgrade action.
- Concurrent-stack correction: after fast-forwarding Hermes's failure recovery, dead-board reweave, and live-bouquet binding commits, the full gate exposed two layout interactions. Exact mobile Round 3 changed from a `96px` objective row to `62px` only when low-move state appeared, and a rare short-desktop post-reweave guide entered normal flow and pushed the `480px` altar below `720px`. The compact two-target row now applies at every move count, while the existing reweave/path-restored cue uses the unused left board-edge slot on short desktop. Neither correction changes mechanics, timing, copy, controls, or mobile guide placement.
- Visual evidence inspected: `work/pass3-desktop-thorn-damage.png` and `work/pass3-mobile390-thorn-damage.png` retain three attributable BREAK events; `work/pass3-desktop-thorn-sealed.png` and `work/pass3-mobile390-thorn-sealed.png` show the completed chip retired in moss/aged gold with `Sealed` contained beside `3/3`. The concurrent `work/dead-board-reweave-desktop-active.png` and `work/dead-board-reweave-desktop-guided.png` show the reweave cue in the left board-edge lane with the complete altar unobscured.
- Verification: project/HTML verifiers, all spec and extracted inline JavaScript syntax, `git diff --check`, and the changed-line credential/tracker/network scan passed. The initial focused natural thorn suite passed `3/3`; the merged failure/reweave/payoff/thorn matrix passed `28/28`; the corrected HUD/reweave matrix passed `18/18`. The final exact merged serial Chromium gate passed `93/93` in `19.5m`, covering the first-three journeys, both economy cycles, all ceremonies, failed recovery, dead-board reweave, live bouquet, Black Candle/Cursed Thorn behavior, atomic reload, low-move pressure, Retry, Shuffle, Help/Skip, pointer/keyboard/touch/drag, reduced motion, 64 tiles/eight rows, images, overflow, and runtime errors.
- Final runtime/layout evidence: ordinary play remains `510` nodes, `84` images, 64 tiles/eight rows, three animated elements, two meaningful progress bars, no dormant prototype scaffold, no horizontal overflow, and no visible broken images. Exact-mobile guided swaps returned control in `379ms` and `489ms`. Full-motion reweave held for `743ms` and reduced motion for `547ms`; both returned to one useful guide without moving or clipping the desktop altar.
- Commit/deployment: gameplay commit `d20b52c3ca0497b7291d83945d76b3c73cd3ad4d` was pushed to `main`. Vercel deployment `dpl_8Y8BdvqjV2ZDXiir7qw6J5hE2VLN` reached Ready at `https://bloom-tycoon-mbxl313th-xerxes-florals.vercel.app`, and canonical `https://bloom-tycoon.vercel.app` was explicitly aliased to it.
- Public acceptance: Vercel passed `2/2` and GitHub Pages passed `2/2` across desktop `1280x720` and exact mobile `390x844`. Each public run entered Round 2 through the existing Next Order action, verified neutral `0/3`, performed the authored triple-thorn swap, sampled three BREAK events, verified contained `3/3 Sealed` copy and quiet accessible semantics through two reloads, then confirmed the sealed chip alongside `Moves 3` with 64 tiles/eight rows, a complete first-viewport altar, no overflow, broken images, or browser errors. ## 2026-07-18 legible live bouquet binding

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html` and this note.
- Player-visible result: the active order receiver is now a readable physical bouquet rather than a tiny dark trough. Its six authoritative ingredient slots use larger original flower heads, visible stems/leaves, restrained empty socket silhouettes, and one central ribbon knot. Earned flowers brighten and dominate unfilled positions; the receiving bloom and binding knot answer a positive objective gain with one bounded `520ms` acceptance beat. The full receiver uses the same deterministic six-slot composition as the reward trophy.
- Geometry and hierarchy: the receiver grows from `138x58` to `184x58` in focused active play without increasing the progress strip height or pushing the altar down. Exact `390x844` screenshots retain all eight complete board rows in the first viewport, one compact guide, clear objective/moves, and zero visible clipping or horizontal overflow. Desktop `1280x720` retains the complete `480x480` altar and board-first hierarchy.
- Authority/accessibility: slot progress still derives only from saved objective counts through the existing `liveBouquetAssembly()` path. Decorative bouquet nodes are `aria-hidden` and non-interactive; target flights and the self-cleaning bind seal remain presentation-only. Reload reconstructs empty/partial/full state from authority. Reduced motion disables the receiver, knot, and bloom animations while preserving the clear static filled state.
- Visual evidence inspected: `work/live-bouquet-desktop-empty.png`, `work/live-bouquet-desktop-first-harvest.png`, `work/live-bouquet-desktop-mid-progress.png`, `work/live-bouquet-desktop-full-pre-ceremony.png`, plus the equivalent four exact-mobile `work/live-bouquet-mobile390-*.png` captures. The first harvest now shows a recognizable Thorn Rose; mixed/full states remain contained and ingredient-distinct.
- Static verification: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, `python3 scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, extracted inline JavaScript `node --check`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Focused browser verification: the desktop/mobile payoff ceremony contract passed `2/2` in `53.2s`. The tutorial plus runtime matrix passed `28/28` in `7.0m`, covering the authored first bouquet and Black Candle lesson, migration, atomic save/reload, Retry-adjacent state, pointer/touch/drag/keyboard, invalid interactions, reduced motion, feedback cleanup, 64 tiles/eight rows, runtime budgets, broken images, overflow, and console/page/request checks. Runtime remained `510` nodes and `84` images with no dormant prototype scaffold; mobile accepted swaps returned control in `535ms` and `860ms` under the serial run.
- Security/scope scan: added lines contain zero credential assignments, private-key markers, trackers/analytics, external URLs, or private IPs. No dependency, asset, round, objective, move budget, RNG, economy, save schema, control, backend, account, ad, or cron change was made.
- Known risk: no player-facing regression found locally. Vercel remains on the prior artifact until its independent deployment catches up; GitHub Pages must be verified after push.

## 2026-07-17 visible dead-board altar reweave

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_dead_board_reweave.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: a real accepted swap whose settled cascade has no ordinary harvest swap now enters one explicit board-wide `ALTAR REWEAVES` phase with the existing cue surface reading `No moves — altar reweaving.` The board dims and rethreads beneath one bounded gold/violet sweep, then returns control with `Path restored` and exactly one directional board guide. Ordinary playable boards never enter the phase, and the retired hidden ritual-log reshuffle sentence was removed.
- Continuation priority: the recovered guide is derived from settled authority in this order: armed Black Candle activation and its full existing lane preview, Round 2 Cursed Thorn adjacency while needed, the first unfinished flower target, then any legal pair. Exercising the guide through desktop click, exact-mobile touch, or keyboard spends exactly one normal move and advances the target/thorn or activates the armed relic.
- State protection: recovery rebuilds only the board. Moves, objective counts, coins, owned greenhouse stages, round budgets, Cursed Thorn positions/HP/progress, and armed Black Candle identity are not credited or spent by reweaving. When an armed relic is the only authoritative continuation it is restored at the same coordinate with the same flower/direction, and its lane guide outranks generic target guidance.
- Atomic persistence and precedence: the settled post-recovery board is saved once before presentation through the existing save function's scoped authoritative-resolution option. The active/guided presentation flags are never saved. Reload during the phase restores the enabled settled post-recovery board, while reload after exercising the guide preserves its one move and earned progress. Bouquet completion and final-move failure are evaluated before presentation; completion owns the payoff ceremony and failure owns `Retry Bouquet`, with no reweave cue, veil, guide, or sequence leak.
- Motion/input contract: full motion holds the phase for `720ms`; reduced motion holds the same semantic cue and static board veil for `520ms` while explicitly disabling tile/seal/sweep animation. All 64 tiles are disabled during the phase and re-enabled immediately afterward. The final serial run measured full phase `748.2ms` and accepted-swap-to-control `1720ms`; reduced phase `547.2ms` and accepted-touch-to-control `1834ms`.
- Local-only deterministic boundary: the focused spec uses `window.__bloomDeadBoardRecoveryFixture` only inside the pre-existing localhost plus `?bloomReview=1` gate. It injects a known settled dead board after a natural accepted interaction/cascade so the rare recovery boundary is deterministic. Normal runtime reports the review gate false and the fixture function is `undefined`; no player control or public QA hook was added.
- Focused browser coverage: `npx playwright test scripts/verify_dead_board_reweave.spec.js --workers=1 --reporter=line` passed `7/7`. It covers desktop click, exact `390x844` touch, keyboard, objective-useful continuation, Cursed Thorn state/HP, armed Black Candle identity/lane activation, reload during presentation, save/reload after recovery, reduced motion, completion/failure precedence, ordinary-board non-trigger, and public-gate absence.
- Integrated Chromium coverage: `npx playwright test scripts/verify_dead_board_reweave.spec.js scripts/verify_runtime_performance.spec.js scripts/verify_failure_retry_recovery.spec.js scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js scripts/verify_payoff_ceremony_contract.spec.js scripts/verify_pass3_feedback.spec.js scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js --workers=1 --reporter=line` passed `82/82` in `23.6m`. `npx playwright test scripts/verify_bouquet_hud_consequence.spec.js --workers=1 --reporter=line` passed the remaining `11/11` in `1.8m`, for `93/93` across all focused specs. Optimized and goal-following desktop/mobile journeys, both economy cycles, Round 1 Black Candle lesson, Round 2 thorn lesson, Round 3 objectives, atomic swaps, retry, payoff ceremonies, low-move HUD, drag/touch/keyboard, and runtime budgets remained green.
- Static verification: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py` passed the project, HTML match-shape, and bouquet assembly checks; `git diff --check` passed. The static hook list now requires the recovery trigger, phase timing, atomic save boundary, guide selection, semantic datasets, reduced-motion treatment, and localhost-only fixture marker.
- Visual evidence inspected: `work/dead-board-reweave-desktop-active.png`, `work/dead-board-reweave-desktop-guided.png`, `work/dead-board-reweave-mobile390-active.png`, `work/dead-board-reweave-mobile390-guided.png`, and `work/dead-board-reweave-mobile390-reduced.png`. Desktop is exact `1280x720`; mobile is exact `390x844`, retains all eight rows in the first viewport, and has no horizontal overflow. Active captures show only the altar cue/veil with no HIT, movement guide, refusal, or legacy narration; settled captures show one useful directional guide.
- Browser/network/layout status: the focused matrix collected no console errors, page errors, failed requests, hard HTTP responses, or broken images. Desktop/mobile retained 64 tiles and eight authoritative rows; exact mobile retained eight complete rows in the first viewport and no horizontal overflow. Baseline runtime remains `509` nodes, `84` images, three animated elements in ordinary play, and two meaningful progress bars.
- Security/scope status: changed files were scanned for credential-shaped strings, private keys/tokens, trackers/analytics, new external network hooks, unsafe public debug controls, dependencies, assets, schema changes, cron changes, and protected-source additions. No findings beyond the expected local Playwright URL/review-gate identifiers. No dependency, asset, round, objective, move budget, currency, booster, backend, account, analytics, ad, or save-schema version changed.
- Delivery: per the implementation brief, no commit, push, deployment, cron, or public-site mutation was performed. Known risk: the recovery is deliberately rare in natural random play, so deterministic browser coverage injects only the post-cascade dead board through the local gate; the accepted swap, cascade, recovery, presentation, persistence, and continuation all execute through the production interaction path.

## 2026-07-17 settled low-move urgency

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_bouquet_hud_consequence.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: the final three moves changed only the objective frame's dark border. The actual Moves counter retained ordinary copy, color, weight, and accessibility semantics, so the final decision was easy to miss even though the deterministic move budgets and failure recovery were healthy.
- Player-visible result: the existing Moves counter now owns a restrained gothic pressure state once a valid swap settles at three or fewer moves. Settled `4 -> 3` and `3 -> 2` decrements receive one brief aged-gold pulse; settled `2 -> 1` becomes the stronger, explicit `LAST MOVE · 1`. The direct counter surface, weight, and color retain the low/final distinction after the pulse. No objective height, board geometry, hit target, move budget, save field, panel, control, or progression surface changed.
- Timing/accessibility contract: urgency is queued only after accepted-swap resolution returns control. Its existing status node announces one polite settled count update, then returns to a quiet static state. Reloaded saves at Moves `3/2/1` render the correct static treatment without replaying the pulse. Reduced motion suppresses scale/brightness animation while retaining the direct low/final counter treatment and numerical truth.
- Outcome precedence: a final-move bouquet completion skips and clears urgency so coin/restoration ceremony feedback owns the screen. A final-move failure likewise skips urgency and preserves the accepted focused `Retry Bouquet` handoff. Shuffle uses the same settled counter boundary, while ordinary mid-resolution rendering and failed/completed objective frames cannot leak a stale low-move state.
- Permanent browser contract: deterministic natural three-matches cover settled `4 -> 3 -> 2 -> 1` presentation on desktop `1280x720` and exact mobile `390x844`, including polite status semantics, pulse cleanup, static reload, unchanged objective/altar geometry, 64 tiles/eight rows, and image/overflow checks. Separate reduced-motion, final-success, and final-failure fixtures prove static treatment and ceremony/Retry precedence.
- Focused verification: the new desktop/mobile pressure, reload, reduced-motion, success, and failure matrix passed `4/4` in `29.8s`.
- Final local verification: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, `python3 scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, all spec syntax checks, extracted inline playable syntax, `git diff --check`, and the changed-line credential/tracker/network scan passed. The complete serial Chromium gate passed `74/74` in `18.0m`.
- Integrated evidence: every deterministic first-three journey retained the authored `6/9/8` budgets and exact economy traces; one desktop and one mobile goal-following seed completed Round 3 on exactly the final move and correctly handed the screen to the payoff ceremony. Runtime remained `509` nodes, `84` images, 64 tiles/eight rows, two meaningful bars, and no dormant prototype surfaces, horizontal overflow, visible broken images, console errors, or page errors. Mobile guided swaps returned control in `394ms` and `486ms`, within the existing ceiling.
- Commit/deployment: gameplay commit `39f5b70ae09146422921dcff0ce8426eb71c9791` was pushed to `main`. Vercel deployment `dpl_FHWwVjQrXR1b97Sri5G9JaX6xN7L` is Ready at `https://bloom-tycoon-o8i210rlr-xerxes-florals.vercel.app`, and canonical `https://bloom-tycoon.vercel.app` was explicitly aliased to it.
- Public verification: the focused pressure/reload/reduced-motion/success/failure matrix passed `4/4` on canonical Vercel and `4/4` on GitHub Pages. Local, Vercel, and Pages playable HTML are byte-identical at SHA-256 `87031e1cd7f51dca7753e2b39750910aa047a9672281ae928a72d87f70bb8e99`; public runs retained 64 tiles/eight rows and reported no horizontal overflow, visible broken images, console errors, or page errors.

## 2026-07-17 focused failure retry recovery

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_failure_retry_recovery.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: failed bouquets showed exact progress, but the recovery surface still leaked Help beside Retry and retry could settle into a board without an immediate objective-useful guide, especially after Round 3. Stale failure/relic/hint state also needed a permanent browser contract.
- Player-visible result: failed focused Rounds 1-3 now leave the 64-tile board readable but inactive, keep exact objective deficits in the existing HUD, mark completed flower and Cursed Thorn targets complete, and expose exactly one non-tile action: `Retry Bouquet`. Shuffle, Help, Skip, stale payoff/restoration, and stale idle hints are hidden during failure.
- Retry behavior: `Retry Bouquet` rebuilds the same round from its authored move budget, targets, Cursed Thorn setup, and 64-tile board rules while preserving coins and owned greenhouse stages. It clears transient particles, selected state, armed Black Candle relic state, refusal/failure narration, and stale failure UI. After retry, the existing board guide immediately highlights a legal objective-useful opening pair; Round 2 preserves the Cursed Thorn lesson.
- Permanent browser coverage: `scripts/verify_failure_retry_recovery.spec.js` covers desktop `1280x720`, exact mobile `390x844`, and reduced motion across Rounds 1, 2, and 3. Each case verifies failed HUD counts/deficits, moves `0`, 64 disabled tiles/eight rows, sole Retry action, no overflow or broken images, two failure-state reloads, atomic retry with preserved coins/ownership, cleared relic/failure state, one guided pair, ordinary click/tap retry and pair exercise, and objective or Cursed Thorn progress after the guided move.
- Adjacent hardening: the existing refusal feedback owner window now uses the shared `INVALID_SWAP_FEEDBACK_MS` constant and tests wait for that window explicitly, removing the previous edge where rapid refusal could restore the prior instruction before the 500ms assertion. The payoff token padding was tightened by 2px horizontally to keep compact ingredient text inside the trophy in the expanded full suite.
- Visual evidence inspected: `work/failure-retry-desktop-r2-failed.png`, `work/failure-retry-desktop-r3-retried.png`, `work/failure-retry-mobile390-r2-failed.png`, and `work/failure-retry-mobile390-r3-retried.png` showed exact failed deficits, complete target marking, inactive readable boards, sole Retry action, exact mobile fit, and guided post-retry boards.
- Verification: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, `python3 scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, `node --check scripts/verify_failure_retry_recovery.spec.js`, extracted inline playable JavaScript syntax, and `git diff --check` passed. Focused failure/retry passed `12/12`; impacted tutorial timing passed `3/3`; recovery/pass-feedback/payoff group passed `21/21`.
- Integrated Chromium gate: the pre-integration run passed `77/77` in `23.5m`. After fast-forwarding the first overlapping deployed Retry-focus work, the complete merged suite passed `82/82`; a second authenticated fetch then found the separately published move-pressure slice. Its exact-mobile low-move contract exposed a real integration defect: `LAST MOVE · 1` wrapped below the two Round 3 targets and raised the HUD from `62px` to `96px`. The mobile low-move flex rule now keeps both targets shrinkable on the same row; that regression passed `3/3` repeats, and the final complete merged suite passed `86/86` in `24.5m`. The run retained optimized and goal-following first-three matrices on desktop and exact mobile, including the vesper-thorn goal-following route, unchanged round budgets/objectives/economy, payoff ceremonies, retry, pointer/touch/keyboard/drag/reduced-motion behavior, runtime checks, 64 tiles/eight rows, and no covered console/page/hard request/image/overflow failures.
- Browser console/network status: the focused recovery spec collected no console warnings/errors, no page errors, and no failed requests across all 12 desktop/mobile/reduced-motion cases. The combined and full serial suites completed with their covered no-error/no-broken-image/no-overflow assertions green. Local preview used `http://127.0.0.1:4173/playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`.
- Security/scope scan: changed files were scanned for credential-shaped strings, private keys/tokens, trackers/analytics, external network hooks, private IPs, unsafe debug controls, and protected-source references. Findings were limited to expected local Playwright URLs and historical notes; no secrets, backend, SDKs, analytics, ads, dependencies, assets, save schema, rounds, objectives, economy values, currencies, or monetization were added.

Mobile preserves the hidden desktop-only label, 64 tiles/eight rows, first-viewport board fit, action focus, no overflow, no broken images, and no runtime or hard request errors. ## 2026-07-17 rapid interaction feedback ownership

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: after an accepted match returned control, its positive feedback continued for roughly another `560ms+`. A fast adjacent no-match attempt could therefore show the new `NO BLOOM` refusal and two red X marks while the previous move still displayed `HIT`, cascade ring/vein, particles, objective flight, and order pulses over the same sockets.
- Player-visible result: committing a second adjacent tile now retires only the completed interaction's positive feedback generation before the new valid swap or `NO BLOOM` refusal speaks. A first selection, nonadjacent reselection, keyboard focus/first Enter, canceled pointer drag, and canceled touch-drag preserve the completed bouquet/order pulse through its natural lifetime. Fast input remains available under the existing timing budget; the independent `880ms` refusal generation and its immutable stage snapshot are untouched.
- Timer ownership: harvest and order-pulse cleanup timers are now tracked and generation-aware, objective-flight seal work validates both round and interaction generation, and the interaction boundary clears prior board narration plus objective/bouquet flight nodes. A stale timer cannot clear feedback created by a newer valid move.
- Deterministic regression: desktop `1280x720` pointer/tap, exact `390x844` mobile, keyboard, touch-drag, and reduced motion now cover both passive input and rapid valid-to-invalid ownership. Passive inputs preserve the same four objective/bouquet pulses without spending a move or changing board/objective state, then allow the pulses to clear on their existing timer. Moves remain `5`, bouquet remains `3/14`, board serialization stays unchanged, and the exact Match 3 cue/hints return after cleanup.
- Rapid valid-to-valid regression: the second accepted move presents exactly one current `HIT`, earns its own objective credit, and retains its current order pulse after the old generation's timer boundary. Focused interaction ownership coverage passed `4/4`.
- Browser evidence inspected: `work/rapid-feedback-owner-desktop.png` and `work/rapid-feedback-owner-mobile390.png` show one readable refusal, two marked flowers, 64 tiles/eight rows, complete first-viewport mobile hierarchy, no prior success narration, no clipping, and no horizontal overflow.

## 2026-07-17 repeated tutorial refusal continuity

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.
- Selected weakness: repeating an invalid adjacent swap inside the existing `880ms` refusal window canceled the first cleanup timer, then accidentally captured transient `No bloom` copy as the return state. At the authored Match 4 lesson this downgraded the visible tutorial to `Match 3 fills the bouquet.` and left the hidden cue stuck on refusal copy, even though moves and the glowing pair remained correct.
- Player-visible result: one refusal generation now owns one immutable pre-attempt cue and glowing-pair snapshot. Any repeated invalid attempt extends the same `NO BLOOM | Use the glowing pair.` response without replacing its return state; the final cleanup atomically restores the exact opening, Match 3, Match 4, or armed `BLACK CANDLE` lesson. ## 2026-07-17 first-action board choreography

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: fresh unskipped Round 1 now has a board-level direct-manipulation guide for the authored opening swap. The guide renders a hollow source halo, destination halo, short connecting track, directional head, and restrained drag token from the current authoritative `.idle-hint` source tile to its destination. It does not add controls, panels, copy, assets, rules, economy, objectives, dependencies, or save fields.
- Implementation/lifecycle: geometry is derived at render time from the two authoritative hinted tiles after the board is built, using their actual rendered centers so gap/layout math stays exact. The guide is `aria-hidden`, has no focusable descendants, and uses `pointer-events:none`. It only appears before the first Round 1 move when the tutorial has not been skipped; it clears on first valid swap, Skip, post-first-move reload, payoff/round handoff, failure/retry states, and any render where the hint pair is no longer the opening pair. Pointer/touch drag hides it immediately through the existing drag-preview board state; canceled/refused input restores the same authoritative guide without spending a move. Reduced motion hides the traveling token and keeps a static connector/arrow.
- Screenshot evidence inspected: `work/first-action-guide-desktop.png`, `work/first-action-guide-mobile390.png`, `work/first-action-guide-mobile390-reduced.png`, and `work/drag-preview-desktop-ready.png`. Observed opening pair was vertical, source `{x:1,y:0}` to destination `{x:1,y:1}`; desktop and mobile screenshots show the guide centered on that pair without obscuring flower heads. Exact mobile `390x844` shows all 64 tiles and all 8 complete rows with no horizontal overflow. Reduced-motion mobile shows the same static direction with no traveling token. Active drag screenshot shows the existing drag preview owning the interaction with no double overlay.
- Verification: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, `python3 scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, JavaScript syntax for all changed specs plus extracted inline playable code, and `git diff --check` passed. The final merged first-action/repeated-refusal/Black-Candle/payoff/feedback matrix passed `6/6`; the broader prior merged matrix passed `11/11`, and four timing-sensitive paths passed `12/12` across three repeats. After rebasing over all concurrent tutorial corrections, the complete serial Chromium suite passed `58/58` in `20.1m`, covering optimized and goal-following first-three journeys, exact economy cycles, payoff/bouquet binding, Pass 3 feedback, runtime budgets, visible and repeated invalid refusal, tutorial Skip/Replay, pre/post-move reload, pointer/touch/keyboard, invalid/cancel paths, reduced motion, Retry, Black Candle, Cursed Thorn, ceremonies, save/reload, images, overflow, and console/page/request errors.

This prevents random cascades or a missed 16ms paint from producing false failures while preserving authoritative state and visual assertions. ## 2026-07-17 atomic accepted-swap persistence

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.

The existing last fully settled snapshot remains in local storage while a swap clears, falls, refills, or cascades; the existing final render saves the complete post-swap result after control returns. - Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.

## 2026-07-16 legible replay reinvestment handoff

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_payoff_ceremony_contract.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_html_match_shapes.py`, and this note.

## 2026-07-16 Round 1 Black Candle guided lesson

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_pass3_feedback.spec.js`, and this note.
- Root cause: the Round 1 Black Candle patch only ran when Bone Star progress was still `0`. Fresh-board variance could partially fill Bone Star or overfill Thorn Rose during the ordinary guided matches, so the next highlighted pair could finish `14/14` before the tutorial ever showed or executed the four-Bone-Star Black Candle move. Reload also did not recompute a mid-lesson Round 1 hint once `hasMadeValidMove` was true.
- Player-visible result: a fresh guided player now follows four authored real swaps through ordinary target matches, then sees `Match 4 makes Black Candle Vine.` with exactly two adjacent highlighted tiles. The highlighted special is a legal player swap that creates a runtime Black Candle Vine row burn. During the burn hold, the screen keeps the focused header/objective/progress, concise `Black Candle Vine! Row cleared.` cue, board, and line-relic sweep visible; the ritual log, payoff ceremony, Restore action, and board-mutating controls are hidden. After the hold, the board hides and the existing ceremony appears with exact tutorial copy `Coins restore the greenhouse.` and one `Restore Greenhouse · 100 coins` action.
- Implementation details: Round 1 now derives a lesson stage from real saved counts and moves, reseeds only the next authored tutorial move, restores staged hints after reload/refusal, and uses a dynamic Black Candle row when Thorn Rose has already overfilled. The save schema is unchanged. The completion ceremony waits on a documented `round-one-black-candle-burn-hold`: `1350ms` for normal motion, `160ms` for reduced motion. The Black Candle line sweep remains visible inside the normal-motion hold. Rounds 2-3 retain their existing move budgets and authoritative move counts; no post-completion move cushion is applied.
- Final correction: removed the hidden Round 2/3 completion rewrite that forced the move counter to one after the player had spent the final move. The honest matrix exposed two optimized zero-cushion paths: `candle-vine` completed Round 2 at `0` moves and `nightshade-glass` completed Round 3 at `0` moves. The replacement is board-path only: when a focused Round 2/3 order has all thorn requirements done, exactly two moves left, and a total target deficit of `1-4`, the existing target patcher writes one legal target swap so the player can genuinely complete with one move left. The existing Round 3 final-move board rescue remains a real legal swap fallback for completion-only strategies; saved/displayed moves are never rewritten after completion.
- Fresh no-review guided traces:
  - Desktop `1280x720`: `0/14 Moves 6` -> swap 1 `3/14 Moves 5` -> swap 2 `6/14 Moves 4` -> reload preserved moves/counts -> swap 3 `12/14 Moves 3`, cue `Make 4 Bone Stars - Black Candle Vine burns a row.`, tutorial `Match 4 makes Black Candle Vine.` -> swap 4 live burn `14/14 Moves 2`, `line-relic-hit=true`, `.board-particle.line-relic=1`, board visible/payoff hidden -> ceremony `coins=120`, one action `Restore Greenhouse · 100 coins`, payoff tokens include `x8 Thorn Rose` and `x6 Bone Star`.
  - Exact mobile `390x844`: `0/14 Moves 6` -> swap 1 `3/14 Moves 5` -> swap 2 `6/14 Moves 4` -> reload preserved moves/counts -> swap 3 `9/14 Moves 3`, same Black Candle cue/tutorial and legal four-Bone preview -> swap 4 live burn `14/14 Moves 2`, `line-relic-hit=true`, `.board-particle.line-relic=1`, all 8 rows visible/no overflow -> ceremony `coins=120`, one restore action.
- Screenshot evidence captured and inspected: `work/black-candle-desktop-cue.png`, `work/black-candle-desktop-burn.png`, `work/black-candle-desktop-ceremony.png`, `work/black-candle-mobile390-cue.png`, `work/black-candle-mobile390-burn.png`, and `work/black-candle-mobile390-ceremony.png`. Burn screenshots are viewport captures; desktop shows the board and Black Candle sweep instead of the ceremony, and exact mobile `390x844` shows all eight rows with no overflow, no ritual-log paragraph, and no Shuffle/Restore control.
- Focused burn assertions now cover: visible non-tile button list is empty; ritual log hidden; tutorial/cue name Black Candle and do not say coins; payoff hidden; board visible; `line-relic-hit` and `.board-particle.line-relic` present. Ceremony assertions then require tutorial copy `Coins restore the greenhouse.` and exactly one Restore action.
- Verification commands/results:

  - JavaScript syntax passed for `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, `scripts/verify_pass3_feedback.spec.js`, and the extracted playable inline script.

  - Full tutorial/drag/keyboard suite passed `9/9`: `npx playwright test scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`.
  - Full optimized and goal-following fairness/economy matrix passed `26/26` with honest authoritative moves: `npx playwright test scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js`. Optimized swap envelopes stayed bounded: Round 1 `4`, Round 2 `5-8`, Round 3 `3-6`; `candle-vine` now completes Round 2 with `1` real move left and `nightshade-glass` remains inside the Round 3 lower bound rather than relying on a display rewrite.

The first-three journey helper now waits for the intentional Round 1 burn-to-ceremony handoff before sampling the coin confirmation.

## 2026-07-16 tactile drag-preview board pass

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: pressing an enabled active tile now gives a tactile drag preview before release. The held flower tracks the dominant axis with bounded movement, the adjacent in-bounds neighbor yields in the opposite direction, and both tiles enter a restrained gold ready state once the existing swipe threshold is crossed. Gameplay state still changes only on release through the existing two-tap swap path.
- Implementation details: drag preview state is presentation-only and uses delegated board pointer/touch handlers. Movement updates only the two involved tile classes and inline transforms; the board is not rerendered on pointer move. Native image/button dragging is suppressed so desktop mouse drags keep pointer ownership. Startup tutorial and idle-hint timers defer while a drag is active, and render/reload/tutorial transitions clear preview state.

  - Pointer cancel/off-board path: moves stayed `6 -> 6`, residual drag classes were `0`; no move was spent.
  - Reduced motion: ready classes are applied statically, source/neighbor computed transforms stayed `none`, and release still committed one valid swap (`6 -> 5`) with residual drag classes `0`.

## 2026-07-16 marathon integration and deterministic fairness

- Reconciled the final four local polish checkpoints onto the remotely published goal-following fairness work without discarding either player-facing path.
- Separated presentation randomness (`fxRandom`) from board randomness so particle/audio tuning can no longer alter flower drops, cascade outcomes, or difficulty.
- Set the focused move budgets to Round 1 `6`, Round 2 `9`, and Round 3 `8`. Across the final deterministic matrix, all optimized and ordinary goal-following desktop/mobile journeys completed with a real cushion; the previously brittle `bone-star-vigil`, `candle-vine`, and `nightshade-glass` routes now finish rather than relying on a zero-move seal.
- Preserved the marathon improvements: compact `?` tutorial replay, deterministic greenhouse ceremony art, faster binding seal, capped priority-aware feedback, lightweight reduced-motion Supreme Bloom, hidden ritual-log narrator during focused play, 465-node/80-image runtime, two meaningful bars, 64 tiles, and eight complete mobile rows.
- Integrated verification: project and HTML shape verifiers passed; JavaScript syntax and diff checks passed; the final Chromium run passed all 24 first-three journey tests plus all feedback, ceremony, runtime, reduced-motion, keyboard, touch, tutorial, save/reload, and mobile layout tests. One stale Round 2 move-count assertion was updated and its complete 3-test tutorial suite passed again.
- Security/IP: no new services, trackers, analytics, accounts, payments, ads, credentials, broad permissions, dependencies, or protected third-party expression.

## 2026-07-15 Round 3 final-swap resolution and goal-following fairness

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: Bloodroot Compact still has seven moves and the same 14 Bloodroot / 13 Sol Rot objectives, but a final target deficit of up to four now receives a legal four-flower closing opportunity. The final swap resolves before failure can be declared, removing the transient `Bouquet Withered` flash that previously appeared while its match was still animating.
- Audit reproduction and fix: the independent `vesper-thorn` route reproduced the reported 26/27 loss on desktop and mobile. Instrumentation showed the last swap had already formed four Bloodroots, but the test and UI could observe `Moves 0` failure during the 165ms resolution window. Failure detection now waits until resolution finishes, and the closing opportunity is explicitly deficit-aware without adding moves or changing rewards.
- Regression coverage: added a secondary goal-following strategy that scores target flowers and Round 2 thorn adjacency but receives no four-match or total-cell optimization bonus. Four independent seeds run on desktop and exact `390x844` mobile for eight additional complete Round 1 -> 2 -> 3 journeys, alongside the existing 16 optimized journeys.
- Fairness result: all 24 deterministic journeys passed. The optimized matrix retained its existing envelope: Round 1 completed in 2-4 swaps, Round 2 in 4-7, and Round 3 in 3-6. All eight ordinary goal-following journeys completed; `vesper-thorn` sealed Round 3 at 27/27 on its seventh and final move on both viewports.
- Verification commands/results: `python3 scripts/verify_project.py`, HTML match-shape checks, `node --check`, and `git diff --check` passed; the 24-test journey matrix passed; the remaining focused Chromium suite passed 11/11 for 35/35 browser tests total.
- Browser coverage: desktop `1280x720` and exact mobile `390x844`, 64 tiles/eight complete rows, all three ceremonies, retry and replay flow, reduced motion, touch/click and keyboard play, no horizontal overflow, no visible broken images, no visible review controls, and no console warnings/errors, page errors, or failed requests.
- Runtime metrics: desktop and mobile each rendered `465` nodes, `80` images, `64` tiles, `8` complete rows, and `2` meaningful progress bars. Mobile guided swaps returned control in `376ms` and `501ms` with no disabled active tiles, overflow, or broken images.
- Visual QA: inspected the repaired `vesper-thorn` Round 3 completion on desktop and exact mobile. The focused Bloodroot ceremony remains unclipped, keeps one dominant `Raise Conservatory` action, and preserves the board-first hierarchy leading into the payoff.
- Commit/push/live status: pending commit and deployment checks at note time.
- Security status: the changed-line credential and external-URL scans passed with no findings. No assets, dependencies, external services, trackers, analytics, accounts, backend, ads, payments, permissions, credentials, or protected third-party expression were added.

## 2026-07-15 multi-seed first-three fairness hardening

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_first_three_journey.spec.js`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: Bloodroot Compact keeps its seven-move budget and existing 14 Bloodroot / 13 Sol Rot objectives, but now opens with two authored four-flower target opportunities. This prevents an ordinary unlucky board sequence from exhausting the order while preserving the Round 1 authored swap, Black Candle lesson, Round 2 Cursed Thorn lesson, objectives, rewards, ceremonies, art, and focused UI.

## 2026-07-15 first-three-order tuning pass

- Files changed: `playable/midnight_bloom_prototype.html`, `scripts/verify_tutorial_progress.spec.js`, and this note.
- Player-visible result: the focused first-three-order journey is less throwaway and smoother. Round 1 now uses the reference-scale First Bouquet targets (`Thorn Rose 0/8`, `Bone Star 0/6`) while preserving the 12-move tutorial; Round 2 is tightened to 14 moves with 29 total bouquet/thorn progress; Round 3 is tightened to 12 moves with 27 Bloodroot/Sol Rot progress. Round 2/3 target weighting was reduced so cascades do not auto-finish the later orders as often, and fresh Round 1 boards now reject any one-move full-bouquet opening.
- Cascade/input pacing: later cascade waits were shortened from the prior 190ms + up-to-430ms settle curve to a 185ms-descending + up-to-360ms settle curve. The player-visible effect remains the same but long cascade chains return control faster.

Completion showed only Restore; restoration changed to `RESTORED · MIDNIGHT BLOOM` and only Next Order; Next Order reached Round 2 with 64 tiles and no overflow. Reload preserved the completed save before restoration, and restoration explicitly saves its state. Lightweight changed-file secret scan: 0 hits. No live deployment checked before push.
- Known issues: none found in the focused slice.

- Security/secret-scan status: lightweight changed-line credential scan ran with no findings.

Exact 4-line still wins as `Black Candle Vine`, exact 5-line still wins as `Eclipse Seed Rune`, L/T/cross shape rewards still win shape rewards, and 6+ or `B`/Sacrifice remains the Supreme Bloom path.

- How to trigger and verify L/T/cross matches without console: after Round 1, watch for the `L/T/cross = Shape Bloom` hint in Round 2; in the review path, click `Shape Bloom` or press `M` until `Witch's Cross`, `Night Garden L-Bloom`, or `Twin Stem Bloom` appears. The hidden `shapeAuditData` verifier still contains L, T, and cross definitions.
- How to trigger and verify Supreme Bloom without console: focus the page and press `B`; the ritual log should show `SUPREME BLOOM! Review hook complete. The board is ready.` after the charge phase.
